subject: Computer Networking
title: Mesh Wi-Fi Systems
description: A 60-second lesson on Mesh Wi-Fi Systems.

hook.text: Dead zones, buffering, no signal in the basement. A single router can't cover a whole house.

buildup.text: Mesh Wi-Fi uses multiple nodes around your home. They create one seamless network everywhere.

discovery.text: Each node talks wirelessly to its neighbors. Your device roams between them seamlessly.

twist.text: A dedicated backhaul channel between nodes prevents speed loss. Without it, each hop halves bandwidth.

climax.text: Systems from Eero and Google Nest manage channels automatically. Setup takes minutes, not hours.

punchline.text: Mesh Wi-Fi blankets your home in signal. No dead zones.

quiz.question: What problem does a dedicated backhaul channel solve in mesh Wi-Fi?
options: It prevents speed loss at each hop between nodes
It increases the range of each individual node
It encrypts traffic between the mesh nodes
